text.title: GitHub Projects Agent Integration and Gotchas
text.summary: Agentic workflow, recommended subagents, GraphQL pagination and permission gotchas, and best practices.

rule.statement: Agents must always resolve issue numbers to ProjectV2Item node IDs before any mutation; use gh project item-list to get node IDs, not issue numbers directly.
rule.rationale: GraphQL mutations require ProjectV2Item node IDs (not issue numbers); passing the wrong ID fails silently without an error response.

rule.statement: Add permissions: {projects: write} to any GitHub Actions workflow that makes project field mutations; GITHUB_TOKEN without this declaration returns 403.
rule.rationale: GITHUB_TOKEN scopes must be explicitly requested per workflow; the default token does not include project write access.

rule.statement: Require human approval before bulk field updates (e.g., setting Priority on 200 backlog items) — there is no undo for bulk project field mutations.
rule.rationale: GraphQL mutations on project fields are irreversible at scale; even a correct bulk update may apply incorrect values if the filter logic has a bug.

rule.statement: Implement cursor-based pagination for all project item queries: check pageInfo.hasNextPage and pass endCursor as after argument in subsequent queries.
rule.rationale: GitHub Projects GraphQL returns maximum 100 items per query; projects with more items silently truncate results without pagination.

rule.statement: After creating a project item via API, wait 1–2 seconds before reading its Status field — built-in workflows (e.g., "Item added → set Status to Backlog") run asynchronously.
rule.rationale: Built-in workflow execution is not synchronous with the API response; an immediate read may see an item with no Status field set.

rule.statement: To find the current iteration, query iteration fields and filter by date range (start_date <= today <= end_date) — there is no "current iteration" API shortcut.
rule.rationale: GitHub Projects API does not expose a "current" iteration concept; date-range filtering is the only reliable way to identify the active sprint.

section.title: Recommended Subagents

list.item: github-project-triage: reads new issues, checks for missing required fields (Priority, Estimate, Assignee), posts comment listing what is needed.
github-release-reporter: queries milestone and project items, generates release checklist issue, posts completion percentage as a comment.
github-sprint-planner: queries backlog by Priority, proposes iteration assignment based on estimate totals vs. capacity, outputs draft for human review.

section.reference: Prompt for agent to triage unestimated project items
